Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones (chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not a pathway-specific determinant; dispensable for reconstructed pinocembrin/chrysin production because endogenous N. benthamiana CHI activity or spontaneous chalcone isomerization was sufficient.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only one CHI transcript was identified in S. baicalensis roots, suggesting no novel root-specific CHI isoform for 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis. 2. In tobacco transient assays, SbCLL-7 + SbCHS-2 produced pinocembrin without requiring SbCHI. 3. Adding SbCHI did not noticeably increase pinocembrin production, indicating CHI is not a decisive specialized step in this pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq transcript identification; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S/HyperTrans pEAQ-HT-DEST1 constitutive expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included experimentally in pathway reconstitution, but evidence indicates it is not a newly evolved specialized determinant of RSF biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones including chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Pinocembrin-to-chrysin branch leading to 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts the 4′-deoxyflavanone pinocembrin to the flavone chrysin by CYP-dependent dehydrogenation/desaturation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of chrysin and downstream 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones; silencing strongly reduces baicalin, baicalein, wogonin, and wogonoside and causes pinocembrin-derived intermediates to accumulate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-2 is highly root expressed and MeJA inducible, matching RSF accumulation. 2. RNAi silencing markedly reduced baicalin, baicalein, wogonin, and wogonoside and caused accumulation of pinocembrin-O-glucuronide and reduced derivatives. 3. Recombinant SbFNSII-2 specifically converted pinocembrin to chrysin, but not naringenin or eriodictyol. 4. Overexpression in Arabidopsis generated chrysin only when pinocembrin was supplied. 5. Together with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2, it reconstituted chrysin production in tobacco leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; much lower in stems, leaves, and flowers; induced ~4.8-fold by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic Arabidopsis and transiently expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase 1 (in yeast WAT11 assay system)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; pinocembrin feeding; cinnamic acid supplementation in tobacco assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi in S. baicalensis hairy roots; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; heterologous yeast microsome enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; Arabidopsis overexpression and substrate feeding; transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive flavones with reported anticancer, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful core enzyme for engineering chrysin and 4′-deoxyflavone production, but requires upstream pinocembrin supply via SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from SbFNSII-1 by strict substrate specificity toward 4′-deoxyflavanone pinocembrin and likely evolved by recent neofunctionalization.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cinnamate-CoA ligase / cinnamic acid-specific CoA ligase
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones including baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside, via pinocembrin/chrysin
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step bypassing C4H to direct cinnamic acid into pinocembrin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA, providing the precursor used by SbCHS-2 for pinocembrin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry enzyme enabling diversion from the standard 4-coumarate route
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient RSF accumulation; RNAi lowered major root flavones, and in tobacco it enabled pinocembrin production when coexpressed with SbCHS-2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-7 is preferentially expressed in roots, matching root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. Recombinant SbCLL-7 showed strong specificity for cinnamic acid and much higher catalytic efficiency toward cinnamic acid than SbCLL-1 or SbCLL-5. 3. RNAi silencing reduced baicalin, wogonoside, and baicalein levels in hairy roots. 4. Expression in Arabidopsis alone did not yield pinocembrin, indicating the need for a specialized CHS. 5. Coexpression with SbCHS-2 in tobacco reconstructed pinocembrin production and with SbFNSII-2 reconstructed chrysin production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in roots; at least threefold lower in aerial organs; not significantly induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in Arabidopsis and transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Peroxisome-associated/predicted peroxisomal due to C-terminal SKL motif
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 4CL/AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; CoA ligase-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment tested; cinnamic acid feeding/supplementation in Arabidopsis and tobacco assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR tissue profiling; recombinant protein enzyme assay and kinetics in E. coli; RNAi in hairy roots; Arabidopsis overexpression; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive flavones with reported anticancer, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important upstream engineering target to create cinnamoyl-CoA supply for 4′-deoxyflavone production; should be stacked with SbCHS-2 and SbFNSII-2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely recruited from a CLL ancestor related to fatty acid metabolism rather than duplicated from canonical 4CL enzymes; critical for bypassing C4H competition.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones including chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses cinnamoyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form pinocembrin chalcone, which is converted to pinocembrin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key specialized isoform for pinocembrin-forming branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for synthesis of chrysin precursor pinocembrin in the specialized root pathway; coexpression with SbCLL-7 generated pinocembrin and with SbFNSII-2 generated chrysin in tobacco leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCHS-2 is strongly root enriched in contrast to flower-enriched SbCHS-1. 2. Its expression is strongly induced by MeJA. 3. Coexpression of SbCHS-2 with SbCLL-7 in tobacco produced pinocembrin, demonstrating specialized use of cinnamoyl-CoA. 4. Addition of SbFNSII-2 enabled chrysin formation, confirming SbCHS-2 as a core upstream enzyme for 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; very low in flowers; induced ~16-fold by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase / chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; cinnamic acid supplementation in tobacco assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qRT-PCR expression profiling; phylogenetic analysis; transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive flavones with reported anticancer, antioxidant, and antiviral properties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential specialized CHS to engineer pinocembrin supply from cinnamic acid; should be combined with SbCLL-7 and SbFNSII-2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Nearly identical to previously reported Scutellaria CHS2 alleles; evidence indicates endogenous tobacco CHS cannot substitute for this specialized function.
